acceptOwnership().details: The new owner accepts the ownership transfer.

addSupportedToken(address).details: May only be called by the `owner` role

params.localToken: The address of the token being used as native collateral
params.remoteDomain: The remote domain for which the collateral is held locally

blocklist(uint32,bytes32).details: This function allows the blocklister to blocklist an address on a remote domain,      preventing it from participating in cross-domain operationsThis function should be idempotent

params.remoteAddress: The address to blocklist as bytes32 on the specified remote domain
params.remoteDomain: The domain identifier where the address should be blocklisted

constructor.details: Sets immutable external contract addresses and disables initializers for the implementation

depositToRemote(uint256,uint32,bytes32,a.details: This function handles the deposit and transfer of tokens to a specified remote domain.      It validates the deposit intent payload and processes each deposit according to the specified rules.

params.hookData: Optional hook data to append to the transfer message for interpretation on the remote domain
params.localToken: The address of the token being deposited and transferred to
params.maxFee: The maximum fee to pay on the remote domain, specified in units of localToken
params.remoteDomain: The domain identifier where the tokens will be transferred to
params.remoteRecipient: The recipient address on the remote domain (as bytes32)
params.value: The amount of tokens to deposit and transfer to remote domain

deregisterRemoteDomain(uint32).details: Only the owner can deregister a remote domainThis function does not clear the remote token mappingsOnce deregistered, a domain cannot be re-registered as it would attempt to deploy to the same address

deregisterRemoteToken(uint32,bytes32).details: Only the owner can deregister a remote token

params.remoteToken: The token address on the remote domain

returns._0: The operator-issued identifier for the current chain
The remote domain depositor address, or address(0) if not registered
The hook executor address, or address(0) if not set
The remote token address as bytes32, or bytes32(0) if not registered

initialize(uint32,address,address,addres.details: Assumes the contract is being deployed behind a proxy and that the proxy has already been initialized using      the `UpgradeablePlaceholder` contract. Ownable is already initialized by UpgradeablePlaceholder.

params.blocklister_: The address to initialize the blocklister role
params.domain_: The operator-issued identifier for this chain
params.pauser_: The address to initialize the pauser role
params.registrationManager_: The address to initialize the registration manager role
params.remoteDomainDepositorImplementation_: The address of the pre-deployed RemoteDomainDepositor implementation
params.supportedTokens_: The list of tokens to support initially

returns._0: `true` if the address is blocklisted, `false` otherwise
True if the remote domain is registered, false otherwise

params.remoteToken: The token address on the remote domain to check

returns._0: True if the remote token is registered for the domain, false otherwise
`true` if the token is supported, `false` otherwise

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

pause().details: May only be called by the `pauser` role

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

pendingOwner().details: Returns the address of the pending owner.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

registerRemoteDomain(uint32,address,addr.details: Only the registrationManager can register a remote domain

params.domainAttesters: The addresses of the attesters on the remote domain
params.domainManager: The address of the domain manager on the remote domain
params.domainPauser: The address of the domain pauser on the remote domain
params.persistentSignatureBufferDelayBlocks: The number of blocks to delay when disabling attesters and increasing signature thresholds
params.signatureThreshold: The threshold of signatures required to attest to a message

returns.remoteDomainDepositor: The address of the RemoteDomainDepositor contract for the remote domain

registerRemoteToken(address,uint32,bytes.details: Only the registrationManager can register a remote token

params.localToken: The token address on the local domain
params.remoteToken: The token address on the remote domain

returns._0: The address of the registration manager

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setDomainPauseState(uint32,bool,bool).details: May only be called by the domain pauser for that domain (obtained from the remote domain depositor)

params.depositsPaused: Whether deposits should be paused for this domain
params.withdrawalsPaused: Whether withdrawals should be paused for this domain

setPersistentSignatureBufferDelay(uint32.details: Only the owner can update the persistent signature buffer delay. This function allows      adjusting the delay for disabling attesters and increasing signature thresholds.

setRemoteDomainHookExecutor(uint32,addre.details: Only the owner can set the hook executorThe hook executor can be set to zero address to disable the hook executor

params.newHookExecutor: The address of the new hook executor
params.remoteDomain: The domain identifier to set the hook executor for

setUnlimitedAllowances(address[]).details: Anyone can call this function to ensure GatewayWallet has unlimited allowance for each supported token.      This is useful if allowances are reduced or depleted, allowing uninterrupted operation.

params.tokens: Array of token addresses to approve. Must all be supported tokens.

transferOwnership(address).details: Starts the ownership transfer of the contract to a new account. Replaces the pending transfer if there is one. Can only be called by the current owner. Setting `newOwner` to the zero address is allowed; this can be used to cancel an initiated ownership transfer.

unblocklist(uint32,bytes32).details: This function allows the blocklister to unblocklist an address on a remote domain,      allowing it to participate in cross-domain operationsThis function should be idempotent

params.remoteAddress: The address to unblocklist as bytes32 on the specified remote domain
params.remoteDomain: The domain identifier where the address should be unblocklisted

unpause().details: May only be called by the `pauser` role

updateBlocklister(address).details: This function allows the owner to update the blocklister address,      granting or revoking the blocklister roleThis function should be idempotent

updateDomainManager(uint32,address).details: Only the owner can update the domain manager. This function allows rotating      the domain manager.

updatePauser(address).details: May only be called by the `owner` role

updateRegistrationManager(address).details: This function allows the owner to update the registration manager address,      granting or revoking the registration manager roleThis function should be idempotent

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

withdraw(bytes,bytes).details: This function handles the withdrawal of tokens from the reserve contract.      It validates the attestation payload and signature and processes the withdrawal accordingly.

params.attestationPayload: The encoded gateway attestation data containing withdrawal details
params.signature: The signature from a valid attestation signer on `attestationPayload`

addSupportedToken(address).notice: Marks a token as supported. Once supported, tokens cannot be un-supported.

balanceOfNativeCollateral(address,uint32.notice: Returns the available balance of a native collateral token for a remote domain

blocklist(uint32,bytes32).notice: Blocklists an address on a specific remote domain

blocklister().notice: The address with the `blocklister` role that can modify the blocklist

decodeDepositIntent(bytes).notice: Decodes bytes into a DepositIntent struct

depositToRemote(uint256,uint32,bytes32,a.notice: Deposits tokens and transfer them to a remote domain

deregisterRemoteToken(uint32,bytes32).notice: Deregisters a remote token from an existing remote domain

domain().notice: The domain assigned to the chain this contract is deployed on

domainDepositsPaused(uint32).notice: Checks if deposits are paused for a specific domain

domainWithdrawalsPaused(uint32).notice: Checks if withdrawals are paused for a specific domain

encodeDepositIntent((uint32,uint256,uint.notice: Encodes a DepositIntent struct into bytes

getRemoteDomainDepositor(uint32).notice: Gets the remote domain depositor address for a given remote domain

getRemoteDomainHookExecutor(uint32).notice: Gets the hook executor for a given remote domain

getRemoteToken(uint32,address).notice: Gets the remote token address for a given local token and remote domain

initialize(uint32,address,address,addres.notice: Initializes the contract and all of its modules, in the order of inheritance

isBlocklisted(uint32,bytes32).notice: Whether or not a given address is blocked from interacting with the contract

isRemoteDomainRegistered(uint32).notice: Check if a remote domain is registered

isRemoteTokenRegistered(uint32,bytes32).notice: Check if a remote token is registered for a specific domain

isTokenSupported(address).notice: Whether or not a token is supported

pauser().notice: The address with the `pauser` role that can pause and unpause the contract

registerRemoteToken(address,uint32,bytes.notice: Registers a new remote token for an existing remote domain

registrationManager().notice: The address with the `registrationManager` role that can register remote domains and tokens

remoteDomainDepositorImplementation().notice: Gets the shared implementation contract for RemoteDomainDepositor proxies

setDomainPauseState(uint32,bool,bool).notice: Sets the pause state for a specific remote domain

setPersistentSignatureBufferDelay(uint32.notice: Updates the persistent signature buffer delay for a specific remote domain

setRemoteDomainHookExecutor(uint32,addre.notice: Sets the hook executor for a remote domain

setUnlimitedAllowances(address[]).notice: Restores or sets the maximum allowance for GatewayWallet to spend supported tokens owned by this contract.

unblocklist(uint32,bytes32).notice: Unblocklists an address on a specific remote domain

updateDomainManager(uint32,address).notice: Updates the domain manager for a specific remote domain

updatePauser(address).notice: Sets the address that may call `pause` and `unpause`

withdraw(bytes,bytes).notice: Withdraws tokens from the reserve contract
